top10.introHtml: Refreshed 2026-07-18. Searched Cars.com (27 found, 20 stored after 2-seater filter), CarGurus (66 found — note: page 2+ returned non-convertibles, stored 15 from page 1), and AutoTrader (55 found, 25 stored after filtering). TrueCar skipped (broken body filter). Top picks ranked by condition, reliability, mileage, and distance from Media, PA.

picks.whyGood: GM's last modern 4-seat convertible — turbocharged 1.6L Ecotec, power soft-top completes in 17 seconds, solid build quality. At 78,750 miles and $12,470 this is strong value for a trouble-free second car.
picks.whatToVerify: Full top cycle (open/close fully), inspect power top motor for chatter, check drain tubes at front corners, confirm Opel-sourced 6-speed auto shifts crisply, look for coolant stains on plastic engine covers.
picks.bottomLine: Honest, modern 4-seat vert at a sensible price — the lazy Sunday cruiser this list is built for.

picks.whyGood: 2015 MINI Cooper — $9,999, 55,000 miles, 8 mi away. Clean title, no accidents reported.
picks.whatToVerify: Oil cooler gasket leak (classic MINIs), coolant level drop (water pump impeller), CVT if auto (not great on pre-2007). Check for rod-bearing wear if S model with 1.6T (listen for knock). Soft-top seams.
picks.bottomLine: Solid pick — get a PPI and enjoy top-down driving for under $9,999.

picks.whyGood: GM's last modern 4-seat convertible — turbocharged 1.6L Ecotec, power soft-top completes in 17 seconds, solid build quality. At 71,211 miles and $11,994 this is strong value for a trouble-free second car.
picks.whatToVerify: Full top cycle (open/close fully), inspect power top motor for chatter, check drain tubes at front corners, confirm Opel-sourced 6-speed auto shifts crisply, look for coolant stains on plastic engine covers.
picks.bottomLine: Honest, modern 4-seat vert at a sensible price — the lazy Sunday cruiser this list is built for.

picks.whyGood: S197/S550 pony car with genuine fun-car credentials — torque from idle, a real rear seat (tight but there), and cheap parts everywhere. At $11,459/96,539 miles, inspect the top latches and rockers closely.
picks.whatToVerify: Cam-phaser rattle on cold start (3-valve 4.6L, 2005–10 GTs), spring perch rust (New England/NJ salt cars), top latch wear (driver's side), rocker panel rust, water staining on rear seat from top-seal failure, 8.8 axle clunk on takeoff.
picks.bottomLine: The real deal: affordable V8/V6 pony fun with 4 seats — just do a thorough rust/top inspection.

picks.whyGood: Retractable hardtop with Lexus reliability — near-zero electrical quirks compared to European rivals. At 124,762 miles this IS 250C should have many years left; just confirm the RHC roof mechanism cycles fully.
picks.whatToVerify: Cycle the retractable hardtop 3x — listen for servo strain or microswitch hesitation. Check for trunk water ingress (drain weep). Power steering pump whine. Otherwise extremely reliable.
picks.bottomLine: Retractable hardtop Lexus reliability at a fraction of original sticker — buy one that's had routine service.

picks.whyGood: Near-bulletproof 3.3L V6 or 2.4L four-cylinder, well-documented reliability. At 66,000 miles and $11,302 this is the kind of car that 'just runs' and needs only basic maintenance.
picks.whatToVerify: Strut mounts (tick at low speed), timing belt (if 3.3L V6 — service at 90k intervals), inspect soft-top seams at rear window, check for roof-drain clogs. Otherwise near-bulletproof.
picks.bottomLine: The sensible sleeper pick — nothing else at this price is this reliable with a convertible top.

picks.whyGood: 2015 BMW 228i — $14,700, 102,000 miles, 12 mi away. Clean title, no accidents reported.
picks.whatToVerify: N20/N26 timing chain stretch — confirm no rattle on cold start. Oil leaks. xDrive transfer case: listen for vibration at 40 mph.
picks.bottomLine: Solid pick — get a PPI and enjoy top-down driving for under $14,700.

picks.whyGood: Audi's B7/B8 Cabriolet shares its platform with Audi's most proven era. At 62,185 miles and $11,480, inspect the door sills for rust bubbles and the soft-top seams.
picks.whatToVerify: Soft-top door seam leaks (common), rocker rust on salt-belt cars, CVT/Multitronic service (if equipped), check for timing belt/chain service.
picks.bottomLine: Solid pick — get a PPI and enjoy top-down driving for under $11,480.

picks.whyGood: 4.2L V8 (B8) or 3.0T supercharged — the S5 Cabriolet is a legitimate grand tourer at a fraction of original sticker. At 69,156 miles, verify timing chain and quattro haldex service.
picks.whatToVerify: Timing chain (V8 is known; 3.0T is more robust). Quattro Haldex clutch fluid — change every 40k. Listen for bearing whine at highway speed. Soft-top seam at rear window.
picks.bottomLine: Solid pick — get a PPI and enjoy top-down driving for under $13,642.

picks.whyGood: Most modern Audi convertible in budget — 2.0T and a solid chassis. At 83,000 miles, this car should have another 60K+ miles with routine maintenance.
picks.whatToVerify: Timing chain rattle on cold start (2.0T EA888 gen 1), DSG/S-Tronic mechatronics fluid age, soft-top drain tubes blocked (common), rear diff fluid if Quattro.
picks.bottomLine: Solid pick — get a PPI and enjoy top-down driving for under $11,999.

manual10.introHtml: No manual-transmission 4-seat convertibles surfaced across Cars.com, CarGurus, or AutoTrader this refresh. Trim descriptions did not include explicit manual indicators for any listing. Check back — manual Mustang GTs and BMW 328i 6-speeds do appear periodically.

mustangs.introHtml: 2 Ford Mustang convertible(s) found meeting the 2005+ (S197/S550) criterion. Pre-2005 Fox/SN-95 Mustangs appear in the main listings but are excluded here.

picks.whyGood: S197/S550 pony car with genuine fun-car credentials — torque from idle, a real rear seat (tight but there), and cheap parts everywhere. At $11,459/96,539 miles, inspect the top latches and rockers closely.
picks.whatToVerify: Cam-phaser rattle on cold start (3-valve 4.6L, 2005–10 GTs), spring perch rust (New England/NJ salt cars), top latch wear (driver's side), rocker panel rust, water staining on rear seat from top-seal failure, 8.8 axle clunk on takeoff.
picks.bottomLine: The real deal: affordable V8/V6 pony fun with 4 seats — just do a thorough rust/top inspection.

picks.whyGood: S197/S550 pony car with genuine fun-car credentials — torque from idle, a real rear seat (tight but there), and cheap parts everywhere. At $12,992/141,000 miles, inspect the top latches and rockers closely.
picks.whatToVerify: MT-82 shifter feel if manual, rear-camera function, top-seal weeping at rear window corners, rocker rust.
picks.bottomLine: The real deal: affordable V8/V6 pony fun with 4 seats — just do a thorough rust/top inspection.
